description: Typed skill graph for the salmon scaffold. Seed candidate lanes lexically or semantically, normalize the entities, then expand through typed skill, platform, and governance edges.

nodes.label: salmon-research-router-skill
nodes.description: Default router for broad salmon ecology, conservation, and management-science questions.
nodes.input_contract: Broad, ambiguous, or multi-source salmon questions.
nodes.selection_note: Seed 1 to 3 lanes, then expand to the smallest defensible connected subgraph.

nodes.label: salmon-entity-normalizer-skill
nodes.description: Seed normalization layer for salmon entities, aliases, and management-unit systems.
nodes.input_contract: Species aliases, jurisdictions, management-unit systems, and common identifier tokens.
nodes.selection_note: Use early, but do not treat this as an authoritative production identity graph.

nodes.label: smn-ontology-skill
nodes.description: Shared Salmon Domain Ontology lookup over published smn JSON-LD.
nodes.input_contract: Shared salmon concepts, term lookups, labels, definitions, and IRIs.

nodes.label: gcdfo-ontology-skill
nodes.description: DFO-specific salmon ontology lookup over published gcdfo JSON-LD.
nodes.input_contract: DFO-specific salmon concepts, term lookups, labels, definitions, and IRIs.

nodes.label: metasalmon-skill
nodes.description: Thin execution wrapper around the installed metasalmon R package.
nodes.input_contract: Salmon Data Package validation, term finding, source role lookup, and ontology fetch helpers.

nodes.label: streamnet-api-skill
nodes.description: Narrow StreamNet REST wrapper for coordinated assessment and related metadata.
nodes.input_contract: Coordinated Assessment table listings, schemas, and record retrieval.

nodes.label: ptagis-skill
nodes.description: Narrow PTAGIS wrapper for telemetry observations, site codes, and metadata surfaces.
nodes.input_contract: PIT-tag observations, site-code listings, report metadata, and validation codes.

nodes.label: rmis-skill
nodes.description: Authenticated RMIS and RMPC API wrapper plus public RMIS status lookups.
nodes.input_contract: Coded-wire-tag release, recovery, location, and catch-sample queries.

nodes.label: dart-query-skill
nodes.description: Catalog and fetch helper for important Columbia River DART query surfaces.
nodes.input_contract: Catalog lookups and important DART page fetches by short name or path.

nodes.label: salmon-literature-skill
nodes.description: PubMed-backed literature search and article summary skill for salmon topics.
nodes.input_contract: Free-text salmon research questions, literature discovery, and compact article summaries.

nodes.label: StreamNet
nodes.description: Platform card-backed source node for coordinated assessment and status or abundance access.

nodes.label: PTAGIS
nodes.description: Platform card-backed source node for PIT-tag telemetry and site metadata.

nodes.label: RMIS / RMPC API
nodes.description: Platform card-backed source node for coded-wire-tag and recovery workflows.

nodes.label: DART
nodes.description: Platform card-backed source node for Columbia River DART queries and page surfaces.

nodes.label: PubMed / NCBI E-utilities
nodes.description: Platform card-backed source node for salmon literature discovery and summaries.

nodes.label: SMN ontology
nodes.description: Platform card-backed shared ontology node for organization-neutral salmon terms.

nodes.label: GCDFO ontology
nodes.description: Platform card-backed DFO ontology node for program- or profile-specific terms.

nodes.label: metasalmon
nodes.description: Platform card-backed package engine node for Salmon Data Package workflows.

nodes.label: stock-assessment
nodes.description: Stock status, abundance, trend, and assessment-oriented questions.

nodes.label: telemetry-passage
nodes.description: Telemetry, migration, survival, and passage questions.

nodes.label: watershed-connectivity
nodes.description: Habitat, basin, watershed, barrier, and connectivity questions.

nodes.label: hatchery-harvest-management
nodes.description: Hatchery, harvest, management, and mixed-use context questions.

nodes.label: coded-wire-tag-recovery
nodes.description: Coded-wire-tag release, recovery, and RMIS-specific questions.

nodes.label: genetics-stock-identification
nodes.description: Genetics, GSI, and stock-identification questions.

nodes.label: climate-ocean-context
nodes.description: Climate, ocean, and environmental context questions.

nodes.label: ontology-semantic-resolution
nodes.description: Semantic, term-resolution, and ontology-boundary questions.

nodes.label: literature-reports-dataset-discovery
nodes.description: Literature, reports, and dataset-discovery questions.

nodes.label: package-metadata-validation
nodes.description: Salmon Data Package authoring, validation, and metadata questions.

nodes.label: auth-required
nodes.description: Meaningful or full access depends on credentials, tokens, or another explicit authorization state.

nodes.label: organization-profile
nodes.description: Terms or workflows are scoped to one organization and should not be generalized automatically.

nodes.label: portal-export-first
nodes.description: The source is portal-, report-, or export-first rather than a clean public API-first surface.

nodes.label: seed-identity-only
nodes.description: The current identity layer is scaffold-only and non-authoritative for production crosswalks.

edges.rationale: Broad requests should normalize species, jurisdictions, and management-unit systems before deeper retrieval.
Stock status and abundance are first-class router lanes.
Telemetry and passage are first-class router lanes.
Habitat and watershed connectivity are first-class router lanes.
Hatchery, harvest, and management questions need a separate lane.
Coded-wire-tag and RMIS-oriented questions are distinct enough to seed separately.
Genetics and stock-identification remain explicit lanes even though wrappers are still thin.
Climate and ocean context remain explicit lanes even though source coverage is still thin.
Ontology and semantic-resolution requests have their own lane.
Literature, reports, and discovery remain explicit router lanes.
Salmon Data Package workflows now have a dedicated route.
Stock and abundance questions start with coordinated assessment surfaces.
Literature is the default corroboration path for stock and assessment questions.
Telemetry and passage questions start with PIT-tag observations and site metadata.
DART is a natural paired source for Columbia-basin telemetry and passage context.
Watershed and basin questions often start with DART query surfaces.
StreamNet contributes basin-oriented assessment context.
Literature fills habitat and connectivity context that the current wrappers do not cover directly.
Release, recovery, and catch-sample data are the strongest current structured surface for this lane.
Literature remains necessary because hatchery and harvest wrapper coverage is still partial.
RMIS is the explicit source skill for coded-wire-tag release and recovery workflows.
Until dedicated genetics or GSI wrappers exist, literature is the defensible starting point.
Until climate or ocean data wrappers are added, literature is the defensible starting point.
Shared ontology resolution starts with the shared smn layer.
DFO-specific concept questions need gcdfo as a paired semantic source.
Literature discovery starts with the PubMed-backed literature skill.
Package validation and helper flows are currently implemented through metasalmon.
Normalization often needs shared semantic grounding without claiming full identity resolution.
DFO-specific semantic questions can require the profile ontology alongside seed normalization.
Assessment retrieval often needs literature corroboration or context.
Telemetry and passage questions often require both PTAGIS and DART context.
RMIS results often need literature context because harvest and hatchery semantics are still thin.
DFO-specific term work should stay connected to the shared ontology layer.
metasalmon package semantics depend on the shared ontology surface.
DFO-scoped package semantics can require gcdfo context in addition to shared terms.
The skill is the repo wrapper for the StreamNet platform surface.
The skill is the repo wrapper for the PTAGIS platform surface.
The skill is the repo wrapper for the RMIS and RMPC API surface.
The skill is the repo wrapper for the DART query surface.
The skill is the repo wrapper for the PubMed and NCBI E-utilities surface.
The skill is the repo wrapper for the shared SMN ontology surface.
The skill is the repo wrapper for the GCDFO ontology surface.
The skill is the repo wrapper for the metasalmon package surface.
The normalizer is a seed alias matcher and explicitly not an authoritative identity graph.
DFO-specific terms should remain profile-scoped unless explicit promotion criteria are met.
The GCDFO ontology is intentionally a DFO-specific profile layer.
Most real StreamNet REST calls in the scaffold depend on credentials.
Many useful PTAGIS calls are bearer-token gated.
RMIS data endpoints require an API key or authorization token.
The current DART skill is centered on cataloged page and query surfaces rather than a broad API-first contract.
RMIS mixes public status pages, docs, and authenticated endpoints rather than a single frictionless public API surface.
